title: Packaged factories
description: First-party @you/* packaged factories invoked with you run --named from the you-agent-factory CLI.
openingSummary: Packaged factories are first-party built-in named factories such as @you/goal, @you/fusion, and @you/tts that you invoke with you run --named.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Packaged factories are first-party built-in named factories such as @you/goal, @you/fusion, and @you/tts. Use this surface when you need the shipped you-agent-factory terminology for choosing a packaged factory, referencing it by canonical name, and understanding first-use materialization under ~/.you-agent-factory/factories.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A packaged factory is a catalog-backed built-in under the @you/* namespace. Invoke it with you run --named <canonical-name>. Built-ins such as @you/tts and @you/goal materialize lazily into ~/.you-agent-factory/factories on first use and stay editable on disk for later runs. Project-local or saved named factories and portable --factory <factory.json> runs are separate invocation paths.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Run a packaged factory by its canonical name with you run --named. Open the CLI documentation page when you need the broader command matrix, and use packaged CLI topics such as you docs packaged-goal when you need per-factory invocation signatures.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is the web reference for first-party @you/* packaged factories. It is not a sync of packaged CLI markdown, not operator-defaults depth, not factory.json topology, and not a per-factory invocation-signature deep dive. Sibling documentation links aid discovery only.
